author: Frontend Works CTO
playwright_tests: flow-d 5/5 pass, theme-bundle-adapter 4/4 pass, flow-a-live 2/2 pass (LIVE Railway, MSW off)
notes: Sprint 1 Loop 4 fix. FR-6 (Flow A live browser smoke — swatch assertion) resolved. Root cause: Loop 3 authored `tests/flow-a-live.spec.ts` line 117 with selector `button[aria-label*="copy" i]` that never existed in the DOM. ColorSwatch.tsx sets aria-label=`color N of 5: hex #RRGGBB, oklch ..., hsl ...` (no 'copy' substring). Copy actions live on <span onClick>, not on buttons. Loop 3 could not detect the bad selector because CORS (CB-002) blocked the browser flow before the assertion was ever reached — the test failure surfaced only after Agentic FB-007 landed the CORS allow-headers fix. Fix: retargeted selector to `button[aria-label*="of 5: hex" i]` and tightened assertion to `.toBe(5)` (expected swatch count). No src/ changes — adapter, actions, api-client, store, PaletteDisplay all verified working end-to-end by the now-passing browser smoke. flow-a-live 2/2 PASS against LIVE Railway (both 'page loads + regenerate + no console errors' and 'network smoke — real /theme/generate returns themeBundle and adapter works'). Flow D 5/5 regression clean. Adapter 4/4 regression clean. Build 0 errors 0 warnings. Doctrine greps clean. Secondary observation (NOT in FR-6 scope, NOT touched): ColorSwatch.tsx nests a <button> inside a <button> (lock toggle inside main swatch button), emitting React validateDOMNesting warning. This is a Loop 1/2 accessibility/validity concern for a future loop — it does NOT affect rendering, data flow, or any assertion. See fix-report.md §Loop 4 FR-6 and self-test-report.md §14 for full evidence.
